title: skopeo 1.3.0 on Alpine does not automatically install /etc/containers/policy.json
body:
This bug seems to have been introduced in 1.3.0. 1.2.1 works fine.

The following command:

```
skopeo copy --src-creds=<creds> --dest-creds=<creds> "docker://<path_to_src_image>" "docker://<path_to_dest_image>"
```

produces the following error:
> Error loading trust policy: open /etc/containers/policy.json: no such file or directory

The error occurred in a Gitlab CI/CD pipeline using alpine. Job spec:

```yaml
Release image:
    stage: release
    image: alpine
    before_script:
        - apk add skopeo
    script:
        - skopeo copy
            --src-creds=${CI_REGISTRY_USER}:${CI_BUILD_TOKEN}
            --dest-creds=${RELEASE_REGISTRY_USER}:${RELEASE_REGISTRY_PASSWORD}
            "docker://${IMAGE_SHA}"
            "docker://${IMAGE_LATEST}"
```

Downgrading to alpine 3.13 and skopeo 1.2.1 fixes the issue:
```yaml
Release image:
    stage: release
    image: alpine:3.13
    before_script:
        - apk add skopeo=1.2.1-r0
    script:
        - skopeo copy
            --src-creds=${CI_REGISTRY_USER}:${CI_BUILD_TOKEN}
            --dest-creds=${RELEASE_REGISTRY_USER}:${RELEASE_REGISTRY_PASSWORD}
            "docker://${IMAGE_SHA}"
            "docker://${IMAGE_LATEST}"
```

Not sure if this is caused by https://github.com/containers/skopeo/commit/c84fc7d2434d09e75fe1cdfdcb7333f5bdbaea1c which was the one thing in the 1.3.0 changelog that seemed potentially relevant.

comments.body:
Thanks for your report.

At a first glance, this looks like an Alpine packaging problem: `make install DESTDIR=$(pwd)/p` does create a `p/etc/containers/policy.json`.

Looking at the package and https://pkgs.alpinelinux.org/package/edge/community/x86_64/skopeo or https://pkgs.alpinelinux.org/package/edge/community/x86_64/containers-common, it seems that the `policy.json` file is nowadays(?) shipped in a `containers-common` package; as for whether it should, or does, get installed automatically, that’s probably best discussed with the Alpine packagers.

(Also I don’t expect this to have anything to do with `--src-creds`/`--dest-creds`; does it really happen only if those options are used? That would be very surprising and something I’d like to investigate further.)

I'd imagine your intuition about it being unrelated to `--src-creds`/`--dest-creds` is correct, I've not tested it in other contexts and assumed that there was a link between `policy.json` and credentials. Alpine packaging issues seems like a much more likely cause! I'll open an issue on https://gitlab.alpinelinux.org/alpine/aports.

Thanks for the swift response!
sorry for the inconvenience, it was a packaging mistake on our end
No problem, thanks for the confirmation.
